g.data-narration: Welcome to Inheritance Basics in Java - a fundamental concept of object-oriented programming.
Inheritance allows a new class to acquire properties and behaviors from an existing class, promoting code reuse.
g.text: A mechanism where a new class (subclass) acquires properties and behaviors
from an existing class (superclass) using the extends keyword.

g.data-narration: Inheritance represents an IS-A relationship. A Dog IS-A Animal, establishing hierarchical classification.
g.text: Inheritance implements an IS-A relationship where the subclass is a

g.data-narration: The extends keyword creates inheritance. The subclass inherits all non-private members from its superclass.
g.text: Use the extends keyword to create an inheritance relationship:

g.data-narration: A subclass inherits all non-private members. Private members require getter methods for access.
g.text: Subclass inherits ALL non-private members (public, protected, default).
Private members are NOT directly accessible - use public/protected methods.

g.data-narration: Java supports single inheritance only - a class can extend exactly one superclass, not multiple.
g.text: Java does NOT support multiple class inheritance. A class can extend only ONE superclass.
// ERROR: Multiple inheritance not allowed in Java

g.data-narration: Inheritance provides three key benefits: code reuse, extensibility, and polymorphism in your programs.
The super keyword accesses superclass members, calls constructors, and invokes overridden methods.
g.text: super accesses superclass members, calls constructors, and invokes overridden methods.

g.data-narration: Java supports multilevel inheritance - Dog inherits from Mammal, which inherits from Animal.
g.text: Inheritance creates a chain: Dog inherits from Mammal, Mammal inherits from Animal.

g.data-narration: When creating subclass objects, parent constructors execute first, then child constructors run.
g.text: Superclass constructors execute BEFORE subclass constructors when an object is created.

g.data-narration: Method overriding lets subclasses provide different implementations of methods defined in the superclass.
g.text: A subclass can provide a different implementation of a method already defined in the superclass.

g.data-narration: Dynamic method dispatch determines which overridden method to call at runtime based on actual object type.
g.text: The correct overridden method is called based on the actual object type at runtime.
// Reference type: Animal, Object type: Dog

g.data-narration: Abstract classes define contracts with abstract methods that subclasses must implement. Final prevents overriding.
Object is the root class in Java. All classes inherit from it, gaining methods like toString and equals.
g.text: Object is the superclass of all classes in Java. Common methods include toString(), equals(), and hashCode().

g.data-narration: Key takeaways: extends keyword for inheritance, IS-A relationship, single inheritance only, promotes code reuse.
For exams: focus on IS-A relationship, single inheritance rules, super keyword usage, and method overriding concepts.
g.text: • Understand IS-A relationship and single class inheritance
• Know benefits: code reuse, extensibility, polymorphism
• Practice using extends keyword and super keyword
